name: skill-creation-guide
description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
capabilities: Follow a 6-step process to create skills from concept to packaged .skill file
Structure skills with proper directories for scripts, references, and assets
Apply progressive disclosure design to manage context efficiently
Use validation scripts to ensure skills meet technical requirements
Package skills into distributable .skill files for sharing
author: notedit
securitySummary: Documentation and tooling skill containing three Python utility scripts for initializing, validating, and packaging skills. Scripts perform standard filesystem operations (directory creation, file writing, zip packaging) with no network access, no credential handling, and no external process execution. All 161 static findings are false positives - the scanner misidentified documentation patterns, markdown code formatting, and standard library usage as security issues.
valueStatement: Creating skills for AI assistants requires understanding modular design, resource organization, and distribution formats. This guide provides step-by-step instructions for building skills that extend Claude with specialized knowledge and workflows.
userTitle: Create Skills for Claude and Claude Code
limitations: Does not execute user workflows or access external APIs
Does not provide pre-built skill templates beyond the initialization script
Does not install or manage skills in the AI environment
Focuses on skill creation structure rather than specific domain knowledge

useCases.title: Build Shareable Skills
useCases.description: Create modular skills that extend Claude capabilities and can be shared with other users through the skill marketplace.

useCases.title: Standardize Team Workflows
useCases.description: Package company-specific workflows, coding standards, and domain knowledge into reusable skills for consistent AI assistance.

useCases.title: Customize AI Behavior
useCases.description: Design personal skills that encode specialized knowledge, templates, or procedures for recurring tasks.

promptTemplates.title: New Skill from Scratch
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a new skill called my-pdf-helper for processing PDF documents. Walk me through the 6-step skill creation process.

promptTemplates.title: Validate Existing Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the skill at path/to/skill and validate that it follows the proper structure with SKILL.md frontmatter, scripts, references, and assets directories.

promptTemplates.title: Apply Design Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: I am building a skill that supports multiple cloud providers. Help me apply progressive disclosure design and organize provider-specific details into reference files.

promptTemplates.title: Package for Distribution
promptTemplates.prompt: I have completed my skill at path/to/my-skill. Guide me through validating and packaging it into a .skill file for distribution.

bestPractices: Keep SKILL.md concise - include only what Claude needs at trigger time, move detailed documentation to references/
Use progressive disclosure - load reference files only when Claude determines they are needed for the task
Structure reference files with one level depth from SKILL.md and include table of contents for files over 100 lines
antiPatterns: Including redundant information in both SKILL.md and reference files bloats the context window
Creating deeply nested reference directories makes content harder to discover
Using verbose explanations when concise examples would communicate the intent more clearly

faq.question: What AI tools support skills?
faq.answer: Skills work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Check individual tool documentation for specific skill installation methods.

faq.question: What is the maximum size for a skill?
faq.answer: SKILL.md body should stay under 500 lines. Reference files can be larger since they load only when needed.

faq.question: How do I integrate skills with my existing workflows?
faq.answer: Skills are loaded alongside the system prompt. They trigger based on metadata description matching the user's request.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using skills?
faq.answer: Skills are passive guidance. This skill contains no network access or data collection. Other skills should be audited individually.

faq.question: Why is my skill validation failing?
faq.answer: Check that SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description, the skill name uses hyphen-case, and description is under 1024 characters.

faq.question: How is this different from prompts or commands?
faq.answer: Skills are modular packages with bundled resources like scripts and reference files. They persist across sessions and can be shared with other users.
